title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Short Term Insurance: Why You Should Rethink It
When it comes to buying term insurance plans, most of you think that these plans are available for long tenures, like 20 years and above. But what if you are looking for a short-term term plan? Is it available?
Contrary to your belief, term insurance plans are also available for short-term periods. In fact, the tenure of the policy starts from 5 years and can go up to 30 years and even above. So, if you are looking for a short-term protection plan, you can opt for the lowest policy term and fulfil your requirements.
Who should go for short-term insurance plans?
A short-term insurance plan is one which allows coverage only for a short period. The period starts from 5 years, and you can choose a term that you like. A short-term term plan makes sense if –
You would be retiring in a few years, and your financial liabilities would end.
You have taken care of most of your financial obligations. The ones that remain would be over in a few years.
After a couple of years, your family members would become independent and would not depend on you financially.
In short, a short-term insurance plan can prove useful if all of your financial responsibilities would end in a couple of years. It would have a limited premium obligation and give you the coverage you need for a short period.
However, if any of your financial responsibility is expected to carry on over a long tenure, a long-term term plan would be a better alternative.
Short-term v/s long-term plans – the difference
Short-term plans are those which are bought for a short period of time, say 5 to 10 years. Long-term plans, on the other hand, provide coverage for a longer tenure, i.e. more than 10 years. In fact, some long-term term plans go a step further by offering you the option of whole-life coverage. You can enjoy the coverage in such plans for up to 99 or 100 years of age.
Besides the coverage duration, short-term plans involve premium payments for a short tenure, while the premium can continue longer under long-term plans.
These are the two main differences between short-term and long-term term insurance plans.
Short term v/s long term – determining the right policy period
Regarding term insurance plans, the longer the coverage tenure, the better. The reason – term plans usually cover death risks within the policy tenure. So, the longer the tenure, the longer the coverage. Moreover, long-term insurance plans prove relevant because of the following two reasons –
Increased life expectancy
According to 2015-19 samples1 from the abridged life tables published by the Sample Registration System, the average life expectancy in India has increased to 69.7 years from 67 years a decade ago.
An increased life expectancy means a longer life span, and a longer life span means that you need longer coverage.
Existing financial goals
Short-duration term plans are suitable only if your financial liabilities are short-term in nature and you expect them to get over soon. But does this really happen?
Even after you have taken care of most of your family’s financial needs, your spouse might depend on you financially. Even in your old age, your spouse might depend on you financially. As such, in your absence, your spouse’s financial security might get jeopardized in the absence of a term insurance plan.
So, while short-term term plans give quick coverage, a long-term plan proves more relevant.
When it comes to choosing the coverage tenure, it is recommended to choose the maximum possible tenure. In fact, if there is a whole-life coverage option, you can pick the option for lifelong protection. This would have a dual benefit –
Your financial responsibilities would be taken care of in the case of premature demise while you still have financial responsibilities. The plan would give your family financial assistance so that they can meet their financial obligations even when you are not around.
It can help in estate planning if you have taken care of your financial responsibilities. You can leave behind a financial legacy for your loved ones with the benefit that the plan would pay.
So, a long-term plan definitely makes sense.
Things to remember when buying term insurance
While you might choose a short-term or long-term coverage, here are a few aspects that you should remember when buying a term plan –
Align the term of the plan to your financial goals. Look at the longest goal in your list, i.e., the goal which would take the most time to fulfil. Then choose the plan’s tenure matching the time needed to fulfil that goal. This would ensure that none of your goals is left unplanned because you chose a lower tenure plan.
Opt for an optimal sum assured so that all your goals can be fulfilled in your absence. Use the term insurance calculator to find the ideal coverage that would meet your needs.
Opt for riders with your base term plan. Riders are optional coverage benefits that enhance the scope of coverage. When you choose riders, you can enjoy wider protection and get assistance in different types of emergencies.
Ensure that the premium is affordable and can be paid over the premium payment term. Missing any premium instalment would call for a lapse, and a lapse stops the coverage and alienates you from term insurance benefits.
The bottom line
A term insurance plan is a protection-oriented life insurance policy that protects your family’s financial future in the case of your premature demise. So, plan your purchase after assessing your financial needs and ensuring that the plan aligns with your goals. When it comes to tenure, rethink short term insurance plans. Such plans have a limited tenure and might fall short of fulfilling your long-term financial goals. Instead, opt for long-term coverage so that all your goals are taken care of, and your family gets complete financial security.
Reference links
https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/indias-life-expectancy-inches-up-2-years-to-69-7/articleshow/92166901.cms
full_pdf_text:
Short Term Insurance: Why You May Consider Rethinking It
When it comes to buying term insurance plans, some may think that these plans are available for long tenures, like 20 years and above. But what if you are looking for a short-term term plan? Is it available?
Term insurance plans are also available for short-term periods. In fact, the tenure of the short-term insurance policy starts from 5 years and can go up to 30 years and even above. So, if you are looking for a short-term protection plan, you can opt for the lowest policy term and fulfil your requirements.
Who should go for short-term insurance plans?
A short-term insurance plan is one which allows coverage only for a short period. The period starts from 5 years, and you can choose a term that you like. Some of the situations where opting for a short-term term plan makes sense –
● You would be retiring in a few years, and your financial liabilities would end. ● You have taken care of most of your financial obligations. The ones that remain would be over in a few years. ● After a couple of years, your family members would become independent and would not depend on you financially.
In short, a short-term insurance plan can prove useful if all of your financial responsibilities would end in few years. It would have a limited premium obligation and give you the coverage you need for a short period.
However, if any of your financial responsibility is expected to carry on over a long tenure, a long-term term plan would be a better alternative.
Short-term v/s long-term plans – the difference
Short-term plans are those which are bought for a short period of time, say 5 to 25 years. Long-term plans, on the other hand, provide coverage for a longer tenure, i.e. more than 20 years. In fact, some long-term term plans go a step further by offering you the option of whole-life coverage. You can enjoy the coverage in such plans for up to 99 or 100 years of age.
Besides the coverage duration, short-term plans involve premium payments for a short tenure, while the premium can continue longer under long-term plans.
These are the two main differences between short-term and long-term term insurance plans.
Short term v/s long term – determining the right policy period
Regarding term insurance plans, the longer the coverage tenure, the better. The reason – term plans usually cover death risks within the policy tenure. So, the longer the tenure, the longer the coverage. Moreover, long-term insurance plans prove relevant because of the following two reasons –
1. Increased life expectancy
According to 2015-19 samples1 from the abridged life tables published by the Sample Registration System, the average life expectancy in India has increased to 69.7 years from 67 years a decade ago.
An increased life expectancy means a longer life span, and a longer life span means that you need longer coverage.
2. Existing financial goals
Short-duration term plans are suitable only if your financial liabilities are short-term in nature and you expect them to get over soon. But does this really happen? Even after you have taken care of most of your family’s financial needs, your spouse might depend on you financially. Even in your old age, your spouse might depend on you financially. As such, in your absence, your spouse’s financial security might get jeopardized in the absence of a term insurance plan.
When it comes to choosing the coverage tenure, it is preferred to choose the maximum term insurance tenure available for your age and profile, also considering the affordability of the plan. In fact, if there is a whole-life coverage option, you can consider opting for lifelong protection. This would have a dual benefit –
1. Your financial responsibilities would be taken care of in the case of premature demise while you
still have financial responsibilities. The plan would give your family financial assistance so that they can meet their financial obligations even when you are not around. 2. It can help in estate planning if you have taken care of your financial responsibilities. You can
leave behind a financial legacy for your loved ones with the benefit that the plan would pay.
So, a long-term plan definitely makes sense.
Things to remember when buying term insurance
While you might choose a short-term or long-term coverage, here are a few aspects that you should remember when buying a term plan –
1. Align the term of the plan to your financial goals. Look at the longest goal in your list, i.e., the
goal which would take the most time to fulfil. Then choose the plan’s tenure matching the time needed to fulfil that goal. This would ensure that none of your goals is left unplanned because you chose a lower tenure plan. 2. Opt for an optimal sum assured so that all your goals can be fulfilled in your absence. Use the
term insurance calculator to find the suitable coverage that would meet your needs. 3. Opt for riders with your base term plan. Riders are optional coverage benefits that enhance the
scope of coverage at nominal extra premium. When you choose riders, you can enjoy wider protection and get assistance in different types of emergencies as per the rider’s terms & conditions. 4. Ensure that the premium is affordable and can be paid over the premium payment term.
Missing any premium instalment would call for a lapse, and a lapse stops the coverage and alienates you from term insurance benefits.
The bottom line
A term insurance plan is a protection-oriented life insurance policy that protects your family’s financial future in the case of your premature demise. So, plan your purchase after assessing your financial needs and ensuring that the plan aligns with your goals. When it comes to tenure, you may rethink short term insurance plans. Such plans have a limited tenure and might fall short of fulfilling your long-term financial goals and can be suitable for your short financial goals. You may consider opting for long-term coverage so that all your goals are taken care of, and your family gets complete financial security.
Source
https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/indias-life-expectancy-inches-up-2-years-to-69- 7/articleshow/92166901.cms
